sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The lateral trauma position is used when a trauma patient cannot protect their airway and remaining supine is unsafe. It can be appropriate even when spinal concern exists if airway protection cannot otherwise be maintained.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.content:
1. Decide whether the airway risk outweighs the benefit of remaining supine

2. Roll the patient into a supported lateral position using enough help to keep movement controlled

3. Position the head and mouth to allow drainage while maintaining the best achievable spinal alignment

4. Continue close airway monitoring and have suction ready

5. Reassess whether the lateral position still remains the safest option as the clinical picture changes

sections.title: Key Points
sections.content:
- Airway patency takes priority when the patient cannot protect their airway
- Left lateral positioning is often preferable in later pregnancy
- Use enough personnel and padding to keep the position stable during transport
- Document why lateral positioning was chosen and how the airway and neurological status responded
